title: Arctic cable bypass pressures legacy route control

news.title: Northern Cable Consortium delays repairs after Baltic undersea sabotage
news.summary: Repair windows widen and carriers admit the legacy corridor remains exposed to repeat low-cost disruptions.

news.title: Arctic Mesh Builders announce financing for a bypass route north of the legacy corridor
news.summary: The alternate route needs far less capital than defending the full incumbent path and promises software-defined rerouting.

market.title: Marine insurance spreads widen for cable-intensive Arctic routes
market.summary: Insurers now price repeat disruption risk rather than a one-off accident profile.

market.title: Orders rise for network monitoring software tied to redundant route management
market.summary: Buyers prefer visibility and rerouting tooling over more spending on static perimeter defense.

dark.title: Ship-tracking anomalies cluster near incumbent repair lanes
dark.summary: AIS gaps appear repeatedly near the same repair corridor, suggesting the route remains easy to probe.

dark.title: Carrier engineers discuss mesh rerouting playbooks in closed operator channels
dark.summary: Operators increasingly talk about bypass survivability instead of full restoration of the legacy path.
